message:
fix(doctor): add ToolCheckMode for presence-only vs probe-execution health checks

Introduce PresenceOnly and ProbeExecution modes for shell tool checks
to eliminate false failures on shells that don't support standard
version probing:

- New ToolCheckMode enum with PresenceOnly and ProbeExecution variants
- check_tool() now accepts a mode parameter controlling behavior:
  PresenceOnly just verifies the tool exists on PATH (used by default
  for bash, sh, git, gh); ProbeExecution runs the tool with provided
  args and extracts version from stdout first line
- probe_failure_is_known_nonfatal() handles edge cases like dash as
  /bin/sh rejecting --version with "illegal option" — treats these as
  present-but-quirky rather than failed
- report_missing_tool() helper extracted to reduce duplication across
  all tool check call sites
- Updated tests to pass the new mode parameter; simplified the
  broken-executable test to use invalid flags instead of broken binaries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
